command.title: Command Lane
command.purpose: Shell-level administration and repair over governed SSH paths.
command.identity_boundary: Named human or agent identities land on ops and escalate with explicit sudo when needed.
steady_state_rules: Use SSH only for command-lane access.
Prefer Tailscale or the LV3 private network over public IPv4 for steady-state administration.
Do not use routine direct root login.

current_surfaces.auth: Named SSH key for ops plus sudo on the Proxmox host.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the steady-state host administration path for operator and automation access.

current_surfaces.auth: Named SSH key for ops on guests, reached through the approved Proxmox jump path.
current_surfaces.notes: This remains the current governed guest path until the full Tailscale subnet-route operating model is completed.

api.title: API Lane
api.purpose: Private-first management APIs and automation endpoints.
api.identity_boundary: Use scoped tokens, API keys, or future client certificates instead of shared administrative passwords.
steady_state_rules: Default new APIs to internal-only or operator-only publication.
Use machine-scoped credentials rather than shared interactive accounts.
Document each sanctioned control-plane API endpoint in this catalog.

current_surfaces.auth: Keycloak-issued bearer tokens validated against the LV3 realm JWKS by the gateway before request forwarding.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the single authenticated HTTP front door for approved platform APIs and the native /v1/platform endpoints.

current_surfaces.auth: lv3-automation@pve token with privilege separation for automation; ops@pam for routine operator administration.
current_surfaces.notes: The Proxmox management API is operator-only and private-first even though proxmox.lv3.org exists as an informational hostname.

current_surfaces.auth: step-ca JWK provisioners with controller-local password material and mirrored root trust bootstrap artifacts under .local/step-ca.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the private internal CA endpoint for SSH certificates and internal X.509 issuance.

current_surfaces.auth: OpenBao userpass or AppRole credentials plus required step-ca-issued client certificates for the external mTLS listener.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the private secret-authority API published through the Proxmox host Tailscale path and protected by internal mTLS.

current_surfaces.auth: Windmill scoped bearer tokens and the controller-local superadmin bootstrap secret for initial verification only.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the operator-facing workflow runtime API exposed only through the Proxmox host Tailscale proxy.

current_surfaces.auth: Bootstrap NetBox API token mirrored under .local/netbox/api-token.txt until brokered identity is introduced.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the private Tailscale-published NetBox API used for repo-managed synchronization and operator inventory browsing.

current_surfaces.auth: Controller-local Portainer bootstrap auth under .local/portainer; governed machine actions flow through scripts/portainer_tool.py rather than broad Docker shell access.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the private Docker runtime operations console and API published only through the Proxmox host Tailscale proxy.

current_surfaces.auth: Private X-API-Key stored under .local/mail-platform on the controller.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the sanctioned internal mail automation API for sends plus mailbox and domain CRUD.

current_surfaces.auth: Private Mattermost admin login today; future operator SSO integration will follow ADR 0056.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the private ChatOps and operator-agent collaboration API surface exposed through the Proxmox host Tailscale proxy.

current_surfaces.auth: Repo-managed bootstrap admin password today; future OIDC through Keycloak once ADR 0056 is live.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the private operator-and-agent workbench. Keep model connectors repo-authoritative and do not publish it through the public edge.

current_surfaces.auth: Controller-local bearer token mirrored under .local/platform-context/api-token.txt and used by the governed retrieval and query tooling.
current_surfaces.notes: This is the private tool and retrieval API that backs the repo-grounded platform context surface for Open WebUI and other approved agent runtimes.

message.title: Message Lane
message.purpose: Outbound transactional mail and operator notifications routed through governed mail identities.
message.identity_boundary: Use dedicated sender profiles and notification endpoints instead of one shared global SMTP credential.
steady_state_rules: Submit platform mail through the internal mail platform rather than arbitrary external SMTP relays.
Keep operator notifications and automation-generated mail attributable to distinct senders.
Treat notification fan-out as part of the documented mail workflow.

current_surfaces.auth: Managed mailbox or service-specific credentials handled by the internal mail platform.
current_surfaces.notes: Stalwart on docker-runtime-lv3 exposes a private submission relay on port 1587 for local platform workloads while public client submission remains on port 587.

current_surfaces.endpoint: lv3-ops-email sendmail endpoint with catch-all matcher to baditaflorin@gmail.com
current_surfaces.auth: Repo-managed Proxmox notification target and matcher configuration.
current_surfaces.notes: Host-originated operator notifications already follow a named endpoint and matcher instead of ad hoc shell mailers.

event.title: Event Lane
event.purpose: Signed or explicitly trusted webhook-style callbacks for asynchronous control-plane events.
event.identity_boundary: Use scoped webhook secrets, signatures, or future client certificates for non-interactive callbacks.
steady_state_rules: Event sinks must be documented and intentionally reachable.
Do not treat arbitrary callback URLs as implicit control paths.
Capture event endpoints that feed automation, telemetry, or approval systems.

current_surfaces.auth: Private internal callback from Stalwart to the mail gateway inside the mail platform stack.
current_surfaces.notes: Mail delivery, auth, and queue events already flow through a defined webhook sink for telemetry and downstream automation.

current_surfaces.auth: Opaque incoming webhook ids generated by the repo-managed Mattermost bootstrap and mirrored locally under .local/mattermost/incoming-webhooks.json.
current_surfaces.notes: These private-only webhooks route Grafana alerts, workflow notifications, approvals, and operator-agent handoffs into the managed Mattermost channels.

current_surfaces.auth: Internal NATS publication from ADR 0071 through the private docker-runtime-lv3 event bus runtime.
current_surfaces.notes: Each observation-loop check emits a structured finding on its own subject so future consumers can subscribe without scraping shell output.

current_surfaces.auth: Internal NATS publication from ADR 0091 through the private docker-runtime-lv3 event bus runtime.
current_surfaces.notes: Each scheduled drift run publishes per-record warn, critical, and unreachable events so portal, alerting, and future reconciliation workflows can subscribe to platform state divergence directly.

current_surfaces.auth: Internal NATS publication from ADR 0102 through the private docker-runtime-lv3 event bus runtime.
current_surfaces.notes: Each scheduled security posture run publishes a summary event plus critical findings so operators and future automation can react without polling the receipt directory.

current_surfaces.auth: Internal NATS publication for ADR 0080 maintenance.opened, maintenance.closed, and maintenance.force_closed events through the private docker-runtime-lv3 event bus runtime.
current_surfaces.notes: Maintenance-window state changes are published on named internal subjects so operators and future automation can see when alert suppression begins or ends.
